evidence: The paper uses permutation feature importance, not impurity-based importance. From Methods: "Permutation feature importance metrics were used to identify microbial biomarkers" and Tables S2/S3 specify "importance score is average permutation performance score." Since impurity-bias only affects impurity-based importance magnitudes (Gini/MDI), and this paper uses permutation importance, the specific bias mechanism does not apply. The paper ranks and interprets magnitude of biomarkers ("The most important microbial predictors for 2-h insulin include genus Methanobrevibacter...") and corroborates with ALE plots that show nonlinear associations independently of the ranking.
